pattern: On an open grid the only thing that matters is who can reach the destination first, so comparing your Manhattan distance to the target against each ghost's Manhattan distance to the same target decides escape.
hints: Detours never help on an open grid; the meaningful quantity is the straight-line grid distance each mover needs to the destination.
You escape only if you can reach the target strictly before every ghost, which is a comparison of Manhattan distances to that fixed target.
Compute your Manhattan distance from the origin to the target; if any ghost's Manhattan distance to the target is less than or equal to yours, that ghost can intercept, so return false.

edge_inputs.description: two ghosts both too far to catch up
ghost lies directly between you and the target
ghost reaches the target at the same time, which is not an escape
ghost sitting exactly on the target
target at origin, ghost elsewhere, trivially escaped
many ghosts, all farther than you
one of several ghosts is exactly tied

stress.description: random ghost swarms and targets across the coordinate range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, min(100, max(1, size)))
    lo, hi = -10000, 10000
    ghosts = [[rng.randint(lo, hi), rng.randint(lo, hi)] for _ in range(m)]
    target = [rng.randint(lo, hi), rng.randint(lo, hi)]
    return [ghosts, target]
stress.note: fully random; both escape and capture outcomes occur
